research.title: Export Controls Hit an AI Model: Vendor-Risk Architecture After the Fable 5 Suspension
research.whyTrending: First-ever export-control suspension of a commercial model (June 12 - July 1). Architecture lessons (model pinning, fallback config, multi-provider) for anyone building on frontier APIs.

seo.suggestedTitle: When Your AI Model Vanishes: Vendor Risk After Fable 5

strategy.angle: The first export-control suspension of a commercial model as a case study in LLM dependency architecture: model IDs as config, degradation tiers, why multi-provider doesn't hedge model-level regulatory risk, and monitoring for absence
strategy.title: When Your AI Model Vanishes: Vendor Risk After Fable 5
strategy.excerpt: On June 12 the US government made claude-fable-5 disappear for nineteen days. If your pipeline hard-pins one model, this is the architecture wake-up call.
outline: Timeline of the first export-controlled model
Failure taxonomy: deprecation vs regulatory pull
Model IDs are config, not code
Regulatory risk in the threat model
